D5 Design L3 Apply Reference

Goethe's Theory of Opposition

Goethe split colour into an active plus-side and a passive minus-side — a philosophy of polarity that opponent science later half-vindicated

Goethe rejected Newton's physics and built a psychological order instead. His triangle places the three primaries at the corners and reads colour as a polarity: a warm, "plus" pole (yellow, orange, red — active, advancing) against a cool, "minus" pole (blue, violet, green — passive, receding). It is a theory of feeling, not a measurement.

Toggle the polarity overlay. Goethe's plus/minus opposition has no instrument behind it — yet the idea that colours come in opposed pairs anticipated, loosely, the opponent-process channels that real neuroscience found a century later.
